content.full_text:
FPPC
Nov | 10 30 AM '91                    JUDGE CHARLES EGAN GOFF
                                      3 MARCELA AVENUE
                                      SAN FRANCISCO 94116


Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Gentlemen:

I retired as a Municipal Court Judge on June 4, 1990. I took
no temporary assignments until July 9, 1991. Since then I have
worked about half-time on assignments by the Chief Justice.
I have not filed a financial disclosure statement since I retired.

Question: Must I now file such a statement, and, if so, when?

                                      Thank You,

                                      [signature]
                                      CHARLES EGAN GOFF

DUDE CHARRED RANCH FOOD
MURPHY CAFETERIA
HAT MERCHANDISE

[illegible text at top]

[illegible text in middle section]

"FOOD GRANT

A REAL MADE DOLLARS"

sections.question:
Must I now file such a statement, and, if so, when?

Thank You,

CHARLES EGAN GOFF

The image appears to be a typed or printed document with some text visible, but the majority of the content is extremely faint and mostly illegible due to the quality and brightness of the scan. Here's what I can make out:

- The top line reads: "SAN FRANCISCO 94116"
- The next line reads: "8 MARCELIA AVENUE"
- The third line reads: "JUDGE CHARLES ECAN COFF"

Below this, there is more text that is very faint and mostly illegible. Only a few scattered letters and numbers are partially visible, such as "080", "0.5", and "3280". There are also some numbers and letters at the bottom, including "04" and "00".

If you have another scan or higher quality image, please provide it for better results.
